_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify and/or determine whether a security incident is indicative of a violation of law that requires specific legal action.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data breach notification requirements across applicable jurisdictions, including notification timelines, content requirements, authority versus data subject notification obligations, and breach severity assessment criteria.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-K-011 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-K-008 (RC, cosine: 0.379), RC-A-005 (RC, cosine: 0.377).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.7/10 (raw 0.371). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [action, determine, identify, incident, indicative, law] vs WIDAI-only terms [across, applicable, assessment, authority, breach, content] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.851 (P=0.858, R=0.843). Balanced P/R — the 19-word DCWF element and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Identify and/or determine whether a security incident is indicative of a violati" and "Knowledge of data breach notification requirements across applicable jurisdictio" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.438 > STS 0.371 (gap +0.067). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.6%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [action, determine, identify, incident, indicative, law]. WIDAI terms: [across, applicable, assessment, authority, breach, content].
